content: Welcome to UofT Triathlon. We swim, bike and run. Join our community of dedicated athletes. About Our Team: Our U of T triathlon team brings together students, alumni, and community members to swim, bike, and run. Our team consists of athletes from all backgrounds and experience levels, from beginners to elite competitors. Whether you're training for fun or chasing speed, you'll find coached practices, supportive teammates, and year-round motivation!

Frequently Asked Questions. If you don't see the answer to your question, please email info@uoft-tri.club. How do I join? Click here: Joining the club. What experience do I need? None really! Joining the club is a great place to start if you want to compete in your first triathlon. Our more experienced members are a great resource if you want to learn more about how to prepare for your first race. We also have a lot of former swimmers and runners who find the club is a great place to transition to the sport of triathlon. Please note: You must be able to swim 300m continuously front crawl to attend the swim workouts. How old do you have to be to join the Club? The Club is open to those 18 years and older. Exceptions are made to current University of Toronto students younger than 18. What's a good workout to attend first? Those interested in trying out the club are welcome to attend one of our Tuesday night coached runs before registering. You'll get to meet our run coach and members and ask some of the more experienced athletes any questions you may have. Do I have to attend all workouts? No, all workouts are optional. Many of our members train with the club and on their own. For example, some members attend the swim and run workouts but do their bike workouts on a comptutrainer at home. Do I have to be a student to join? No, you just need to be a member of the University of Toronto Athletic Centre. AC membership is open to students, faculty, alumni and members of the community. Many of our members are Grad students, Alumni or working professionals. How do I reserve a spot in the spin class? Spots are reserved on the forum. You can request access to the forum on our website. Once you've requested access, we will confirm that you are a member and you will receive an email with your login details. Do you offer swim practices longer than 1 hour? Our swim practices are 2 hours long again! What equipment do I need? The only real equipment you need is a good pair of running shoes and goggles for swim workouts. If you want to attend the outdoor bike workouts, you will need your own bike. We also recommend members get some training tools for swimming such as a pull buoy, paddles, or fins. Do I have to race? No. While we encourage members to race, it is not required. The sport of triathlon is a great way to get in shape or exercise with a group in a fun atmosphere. Students can also compete in the University Multisport Series and travel to events organized by other Ontario University clubs. When do sessions start and end? Can I join in the middle of a session? We operate 12 months a year and offer 3 sessions. Fall (Sept-Dec), Winter (Jan-Apr), Spring (May – Jun), and Summer (Jul – Aug). You may join while the session is in progress. It is the AC's policy to not pro-rate fees. How many workouts do members generally attend in a week? On average, members attend 2-3 workouts per week. But for optimal performance you may need to do additional non-official workouts which you may want to schedule with other members that you meet. If I just want to swim and bike, can I select the Duathlon Package? The Duathlon Package is for biking/spinning and running only. The reason that we offer this at a reduced fee is because the majority of our coaching costs go towards swim coaching.

Join Us! Our Goal: To promote triathlon to the University of Toronto community through swim, bike, and run workouts which are both fun and challenging. Who Can Join? The University of Toronto Triathlon Club is open to students, alumni, faculty, and community members of the U of T Athletic Centre who are 18 years and older (exceptions are made for current U of T students who are 17.) We welcome athletes of all abilities from experienced triathletes to those new to the sport. The club operates year round, offering professionally coached swim and run workouts and member-led bike/spin workouts. Athlete Types: Recreational Athletes - Those who are new to endurance sports and are primarily interested in triathlon training to get back in shape. Short Course Athletes - Those who have some experience in endurance sports and are primarily interested in competing in Sprint and Olympic distance triathlons/duathlons or 5k/10k running races. Long Course Athletes - Those who have experience in endurance sports and are primarily interested in competing in Long Course to Ironman distance triathlons or half-marathon/marathon running races. Beginners Please Note: You must be able to swim 300m continuous before you attend the swim workouts. If you are new to swimming, the AC offers various swim classes to get you started. How to Join - Step by step: 1) Try Us Out - Attend any one of our workouts to meet us and try it out! For indoor spin workouts, email info@uoft-tri.club to make sure there is a bike reserved for you. 2) Join the U of T Athletics Centre (AC) - You must be an AC-member to join the Tri Club (provides access to training facilities, including pool). U of T students are automatically AC-members during the Fall and Winter terms. Otherwise, AC-membership can be purchased at the AC Main Office. 3) Join the Tri Club - To register for the Tri Club go to recreation.utoronto.ca or register in person at the AC Main Office. There are 3 packages available: Triathlon (Swim, Run + Spin Workouts), Duathlon (Run + Spin Workouts), Run only. Fees: Full Tri $256 + HST, Half Tri $136 + HST, Full Du $213 + HST, Half Du $122 + HST, Full Run $182 + HST, Half Run $101 + HST. Register at recreation.utoronto.ca - Club Sports: Triathlon Club. You must have an AC membership to join the Triathlon Club. 4) Create an account on this website and email info@uoft-tri.club your membership receipt to get approved as a member here. Then you will be able to sign up for spin and brick workouts on the forum. 5) Come to the workouts! For all other inquiries or questions please email info@uoft-tri.club. Team Charter: The University of Toronto Triathlon Club Charter. The club is open to all individuals who are members of the Athletic Center. You must be 18 years old, unless you are a 17 year old student. The UofT Tri Club is run by an executive team. Contact info@uoft-tri.club or reach out through social media. Executive Team positions: President, Treasurer, Secretary, Webmaster, Social coordinator and Social Media Manager. Requirements: Members who joined for the full triathlon option must be able to swim 300 meter continuous Front Crawl in under 10 minutes and 30 seconds. Swim conduct: Respect pool staff, lanes by skill/speed, circle swim, be on time (10 min late = late), follow workouts, pick up items. Bike/Spin: Sign up on forum for indoor spins, limited bikes. No show policy: 2+ no-shows (without 24h cancel) = 1 week suspension. Outdoor rides: need own bike, helmet required. Run: Track runs coached, group tempo runs social. Contact info@uoft-tri.club for questions.

Workout Schedule. Spring (April 29 - June 25), Summer (June 25 - August 20), Fall/Winter (September 1 - April 29). Weekly schedule: Monday - Outdoor Ride or Spin. Tuesday - Swim 8:30-10:30am (Spring/Fall) or 7-9am (Summer), Track Run 6:15pm. Wednesday - Outdoor Ride or Spin. Thursday - Swim, Tempo Run 6:15pm. Friday - Brick 6:30-8pm (Fall/Winter). Saturday - Group Ride. Sunday - Swim 10:00-12:00pm. Where are the practices? Swims: Varsity Pool, UofT Athletic Centre, 55 Harbord St, Toronto, ON M5S 2W6. Spins: Field House – Court 4 (West side), UofT Athletic Centre, 55 Harbord St, Toronto, ON M5S 2W6. Bricks (Spin and Run): Field House – Court 4 (West side), UofT Athletics Centre, 55 Harbord St, Toronto, ON M5S 2W6. Tuesday Track Run: Central Tech Track, 725 Bathurst St, Toronto, ON M5S 2R5. Thursday Tempo Run: Meet in the lobby of the Athletic Centre. For outdoor rides, check the forum for exact times and locations. Sign up for Spins and Bricks on the Forum.
App Support. If you need help or need to report a bug, please contact info@uoft-tri.club.

Privacy Policy. This policy explains what information we collect through the UofT Triathlon website and app, how we use it, and your choices. Contact info@uoft-tri.club. Information we collect: Account details (name, email, phone, role, membership term), activity data (workout/event signups, attendance, posts), device and security (login info, push tokens). How we use it: Account management, scheduling, notifications, security, service improvement. We don't sell data or use it for third-party advertising. Sharing: Only with service providers under restricted agreements. Data retention: Passwords hashed, data kept while account active. Request deletion by emailing info@uoft-tri.club. Your choices: Access/update profile, request deletion, control notifications. Contact: info@uoft-tri.club.
Resources. Training plans, guides, and helpful links will appear here. Contact info@uoft-tri.club for more information.
